revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ajiko-jinja]][[ja:阿治古神社]] {{Expand Japanese|阿治古神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ajiko Shrine | native_name = 阿治古神社 | image = Ajiko-jinja ac (1).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ajiko Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.047056|139.091194}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Ajiko Shrine'''|阿治古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * Province|ja|伊豆国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 35 ** P155: {{ill|Minamikono Shrine|lt=Minamikono Shrine|qid=Q135039658|1=Minamikono Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039660|ltq=Q135039660|qid=Q135039660|1=Ihatewakeno- Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Atami|lt=Atami|ar|أتامي (شيزوكا)|arz|اتامى|azb|آتامی، شیزوئوکا|ceb|Atami (lungsod sa Hapon)|ce|Атами|cs|Atami (Šizuoka)|cy|Atami|de|Atami (Shizuoka)|en|Atami|envoyage|Atami|es|Atami|et|Atami|eu|Atami|fa|آتامی، شیزوئوکا|fi|Atami|fr|Atami|frvoyage|Atami|gl|Atami|he|אטאמי|hy|Աթամի|id|Atami, Shizuoka|it|Atami|ja|熱海市|javoyage|熱海市|ko|아타미시|lt|Atamis|mg|Atami|ms|Atami, Shizuoka|mzn|آتامی، شیزوئوکا|nl|Atami|pl|Atami|pt|Atami|ro|Atami|ru|Атами|runews|Категория:Атами|sv|Atami|svvoyage|Atami|tg|Атами|th|อาตามิ|tl|Atami|tr|Atami|tt|Атами|tum|Atami|uk|Атамі (Сідзуока)|vi|Atami, Shizuoka|war|Atami, Shizuoka|zh_classical|熱海市|zh_min_nan|Atami-chhī|zh_yue|熱海|zh|熱海市|qid=Q653364|1=Atami}} * {{ill|Izu Province|lt=Izu Province|ar|مقاطعة إزو|be|Ідзу (правінцыя)|ckb|پارێزگای ئیزو|cs|Provincie Izu|cv|Идзу|de|Provinz Izu|en|Izu Province|es|Provincia de Izu|fa|ولایت ایزو|fr|Province d'Izu|gl|Provincia de Izu|hak|Yî-theu-koet|id|Provinsi Izu|it|Izu (provincia)|ja|伊豆国|ko|이즈국|lt|Idzu žemė|nl|Izu (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Izu|pt|Província de Izu|ru|Идзу (провинция)|simple|Izu Province|sk|Izu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Изу|th|แคว้นอิซุ|tr|Izu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Ідзу|zh_min_nan|Izu-kok|zh_yue|伊豆國|zh|伊豆國|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}} * {{ill|Q961511|ltq=Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}} == coordinate location (P625) == * P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == postal code (P281) == * 413-0103https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5080105003778 == Commons category (P373) == * Ajiko-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Achiko Shrine|lt=Achiko Shrine|qid=Q135039659|1=Achiko Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県熱海市網代172', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都大島町野増', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ajiko-jinja}} [[JA:阿治古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q110858812}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ajiko Shrine
Ajiko Jinja |Image = [[File:Ajiko-jinja ac (1).jpg|250px]]
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tairyo-zakura|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=TAIRYOZAKURA|1=UNKNOWN}}) and the main sanctuary
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|02|49.4|N|139|05|28.3|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|qid=Q653364|1=Atami}}{{ill|lt=Ajiro (Atami City)|Q11607160|qid=Q11607160|lt=Ajiro|1=Ajiro}} 172 |Coordinates = {{coord|35|02|49.4|N|139|05|28.3|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|lt=Amenotajikarao|Q849412|qid=Q849412|lt=Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} candidate shrine |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = July 19–21 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ajiko Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|02|49.4|N|139|05|28.3|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Ajiko Shrine''' (Ajiko Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ajiro (Atami City)|Q11607160|qid=Q11607160|lt=Ajiro|1=Ajiro}}, {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|qid=Q653364|1=Atami}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is considered a candidate shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|lt=former village shrine|1=Son-sha}}. The main annual festival is held from July 19 to July 21, and the Ajiko Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashima Odori|1=UNKNOWN}} performed during the festival is designated as an intangible cultural property of Atami City. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * {{ill|lt=Amenotajikarao|Q849412|qid=Q849412|lt=Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} (Amenotajikarao) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} (Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto) (Note: On {{ill|lt=Izu Oshima|Q1070580|qid=Q1070580|1=Izu Ōshima}}, there is {{ill|lt=Omiya Shrine (Oshima Town)|Q131925939|qid=Q131925939|lt=Omiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine}}—whose former name before relocation due to volcanic eruption was Ajiko Shrine—where Ajiko-no-Mikoto, the eldest son of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|qid=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyojin|1=Mishima Taisha}} and “Habun no Oho-ugo” (Hafuhime-no-Mikoto), is enshrined. Therefore, it is possible that the Ajiko Shrine in Ajiro once functioned as an evacuation branch shrine or a distant worship site, though the relationship between the two shrines is currently unclear.) === History === The founding year is unknown. It is identified as Ajiko Shrine listed among the {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province|Q11380552|qid=Q11380552|lt=Shikinaisha|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, located in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}. However, another prominent candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Omiya Shrine (Oshima Town)|Q131925939|qid=Q131925939|lt=Omiya Shrine|1=Omiya Shrine}} on {{ill|lt=Izu Oshima|Q1070580|qid=Q1070580|1=Izu Ōshima}}, which enshrines Ajiko-no-Mikoto. Originally, the shrine was located on Mt. Asahi to the south of the Ajiro settlement, but it was relocated to its present site closer to the village sometime before 1647 (Shoho 3).“Yui-shogaki,” Ajiko Shrine The Takasugi family, who serve as the shrine priests of Ajiko Shrine, also historically served as priests of {{ill|lt=Hatsuki Shrine|Q119929623|qid=Q119929623|1=Hatsuki Shrine}} on nearby {{ill|lt=Hatsushima|Q689643|qid=Q689643|1=Hatsushima}} to the east. The family possesses the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Goshintai|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Kakebotoke|Q108461401|qid=Q108461401|1=kakebotoke}}) of Hatsuki Shrine, believed to date back to the Kamakura period.[https://www.hatsushima.jp/find/history.html History of Hatsushima] – Fuji Kyuko Marine Resort (Today, the rituals of Hatsuki Shrine are conducted by {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|qid=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}}, which is connected through the myth of Hatsukihime.[https://www.hatsushima.jp/find/legend.html Legends of Hatsushima] – Fuji Kyuko Marine Resort[https://atamii.jp/today/113728/ Hatsushima / Hatsuki Shrine annual festival, Kashima Odori dedicated, praying for island safety and a good catch] – Atami Net Newspaper, 2019/7/19) In 1591 (Tensho 18), {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} carried out the {{ill|lt=Odawara Campaign|Q2622890|qid=Q2622890|1=Siege of Odawara (1590)}}. Hideyoshi ordered Ajiro Village to provide military ships to attack Atsugi Castle, and 30 vessels were dispatched from Ajiro. After Atsugi Castle fell successfully, Hideyoshi rewarded Ajiko Shrine, granting permission to use banners dyed with the “Nagare-hyotan” (flowing gourd) pattern and to wear swords. Even today, during festivals, the sacred boat “Ryomiyamaru” (Ryomiyamaru) and its attendants use banners bearing the flowing gourd emblem. Furthermore, it is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|qid=Q314481|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, when the shogunate’s ceremonial ship {{ill|lt=Atakamaru|Q11450528|qid=Q11450528|1=Atake Maru}} was towed to {{ill|lt=Edo|Q215646|qid=Q215646|1=Edo}}, the Tokugawa shogunate granted permission to use “white streamers.” On September 1, 1923 (Taisho 12), the {{ill|lt=Great Kanto Earthquake|Q3742341|qid=Q3742341|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake}} occurred, causing the shrine buildings of Ajiko Shrine to collapse. In July 1926 (Taisho 15), the current shrine buildings were completed at a cost of 35,000 yen. In 2020 (Reiwa 2), the annual festival was canceled due to the impact of the {{ill|lt=COVID-19 pandemic|Q81068910|qid=Q81068910|lt=global outbreak of COVID-19|1=COVID-19 pandemic}}. In 2021 (Reiwa 3), it was canceled for a second consecutive year for the same reason. === Festivals === The annual festival is held every year from July 19 to July 21 (or the nearest weekend), with the 19th as the eve festival, the 20th as the main festival, and the 21st as the concluding festival. On the final day, the “Ajiro Bay Festival” is held at {{ill|lt=Ajiro Fishing Port|Q11607165|qid=Q11607165|lt=Ajiro Fishing Port|1=Port of Ajiro}}.[https://atamii.jp/today/御神船「両宮丸」２０日に網代を渡御-２１日にべ/ Sacred boat “Ryomiyamaru” parades through Ajiro on the 20th, Bay Festa on the 21st – Atami Ajiko Shrine annual festival] – Atami Net Newspaper, 2019/7/18 During the annual festival, a divine procession takes place in which the sacred boat “Ryomiyamaru,” carrying a {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|qid=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}}, parades through the town of Ajiro.[https://atamii.jp/today/113771/ Sacred boat “Ryomiyamaru” parades valiantly through Ajiro, resounding with shouts of “Yaa-Yaa”] Atami Net Newspaper, July 20, 2019 Banners dyed with the “Nagare-hyotan” flowing gourd pattern, permitted by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, and “white streamers” permitted by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|1=UNKNOWN}} are used. The procession is also simply called “the Ajiro Festival.” The Ajiko Shrine Kashima Odori is also performed during the annual festival, but writing down the song lyrics is forbidden, and they are passed down orally. Along the eastern coast of the {{ill|lt=Izu Peninsula|Q822822|qid=Q822822|1=Izu Peninsula}}, there are many regions that perform {{ill|lt=Kashima Odori|Q11677163|qid=Q11677163|1=kashima odo}}. === Shrine grounds === [[File:Ajiko-jinja ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii gate and shrine grounds]] * Main sanctuary * Worship hall * Torii gate === Subsidiary shrines === * Kaizumi Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Inari Shrine * Asama Shrine * Konpira Shrine === Flora === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tairyo-zakura|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=TAIRYOZAKURA|1=UNKNOWN}}) – Donated by people involved in the fishing industry around 1998 (Heisei 10) and two trees are planted beside the main sanctuary.[https://atamii.jp/today/146883/ “Tairyo-zakura” in full bloom, fishing town turns cherry-red – Ajiro, Ajiko Shrine] – Atami Net Newspaper, 2022/3/14 === Cultural properties === === Atami City Intangible Cultural Properties === * Ajiko Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashima Odori|1=UNKNOWN}} === Visitor information === ; Address * 413-0103 172 Ajiro, Atami City, Shizuoka Prefecture ; Access * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Ajiro Station|Q4699760|qid=Q4699760|1=Ajiro Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Ito Line|Q1049129|qid=Q1049129|1=Itō Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|qid=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}}. * {{ill|lt=Tokai Bus|Q11527778|qid=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}}: Get off at the “Miyamachi” bus stop, then walk about 1 minute, looping around from the north. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Ajiro Fishing Port|Q11607165|qid=Q11607165|1=Port of Ajiro}} === External links === {{commonscat|Ajiko-jinja}} * [https://www.nihon-kankou.or.jp/shizuoka/222054/detail/22205be2220095124 Ajiko Shrine Kashima Odori] Japan Travel and Tourism Association {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ajiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ajiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ajiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:熱海市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Atami]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]]
